name: Lowry State Bank
description_short: Lowry State Bank is a locally-owned, family-operated community bank founded in 1899, offering FDIC-insured deposit accounts, CDs, and online banking services.
description_long: Lowry State Bank was officially chartered on December 26, 1907, and has operated continuously for over 125 years as an independent, locally-owned institution. The bank absorbed the Farmers and Merchants State Bank of Lowry in 1932 and has remained rooted in small-town banking philosophy throughout its history. Ownership transitioned through several families (Engebretson, Mork, and currently Nelson/Martinson group as of 2002) while maintaining its commitment to community-focused service. The current market president is Wendy Wesbur, appointed in 2025. Lowry State Bank offers traditional retail banking products including checking and savings accounts, Certificates of Deposit (CDs), and online banking with account enrollment and login capabilities. As of December 2025, they feature promotional CD rates: 7-Month CDs at 3.75% APY (3.70% interest rate) and 11-Month CDs at 3.50% APY (3.45% interest rate), both requiring a $500 minimum deposit. All deposits are FDIC-insured, backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. The bank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on personal relationships and local decision-making as a small-town community bank. It actively participates in community engagement, including a Non-Profit Giveaway program awarding $500 donations to five non-profits serving their communities (annual application window in April). The board of directors consists entirely of local stakeholders: David Lorence (Chairman), Dennis Martinson, Paul Martinson, Peter Nelson, Eric Nelson, and Kari Nelson. Leadership emphasizes treating customers "in a way that we ourselves would want to be treated." Lowry State Bank is best suited for customers seeking traditional community banking with personal service and FDIC protection rather than competitive rates or extensive product offerings. Limited information is available regarding loan products, investment services, or other specialized offerings. The institution appears focused primarily on deposit products and online banking convenience for small-town communities.
services: Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with promotional rates
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Over 125 years of continuous operation since 1899 demonstrates institutional stability
Locally-owned and family-operated with board members deeply rooted in the community
Competitive CD rates as of December 2025 (7-month at 3.75% APY, 11-month at 3.50% APY)
Active community involvement through non-profit funding program and sponsorships
Online banking platform available with account enrollment and login capabilities
Low CD minimum deposit requirement of $500
cons: Limited product information available—no details on checking account features, fee structures, or loan offerings
Small-town bank may have limited branch network and ATM access compared to regional/national banks
Website lacks transparency on account requirements, monthly fees, overdraft policies, and rate change procedures
No information provided about business banking, investment services, credit cards, or mortgages
Promotional CD rates show early withdrawal penalties but terms and penalty amounts are not specified
best_for: Small-town residents seeking traditional banking with personal relationships and local decision-making
Customers prioritizing FDIC safety and community bank stability over competitive national rates
Non-profit organizations serving Lowry-area communities eligible for annual donation program
diagnosis: Lowry State Bank is best for small-town residents and community organizations seeking traditional, relationship-focused banking with FDIC protection and local ownership. The main caveat is that detailed product information, competitive rates, loan options, and fee structures are not available on their website, requiring direct contact for comprehensive account details.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
